code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or anger
    - Product is defective, unsafe, or immediately returned
    - Safety hazards automatically warrant 1 star
    - Explicit negative declarations: "terrible," "junk," "don't buy"

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - More negative than positive
    - Core functionality compromised by major flaws
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"
    - Pattern: "Works BUT [serious issue]..."

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros and cons
    - Conditional utility: "good for occasional use"
    - Temporary solution: "better than nothing"
    - Requires significant user effort to achieve basic functionality

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Clear satisfaction with specific criticisms
    - Explicit star deductions for usability/durability issues
    - Concerns about construction methods/longevity despite current functionality
    - Example: "Deducting one star for dishwasher safety but still recommend"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Aesthetic appeal combined with functional excellence
    - Explicit "5 stars overall" declarations override specific critiques
    - Multi-feature praise outweighs isolated minor issues
    - Transformative benefits: "exceeded expectations," "life-changing"

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Star deductions for non-core issues + strong recommendation →5 ("Still 5 stars despite...")
    - Durability concerns without immediate failure →4
    - Functional deficiencies requiring user fixes →4 if improved, →3 if persistent
    - Safety issues override all positives →1
    - Price praise with "worth every penny" phrasing →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: This mattress gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make it exceptional!
<Reasoning>Explicit multi-aspect evaluation with overriding positive conclusion.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: The color pattern is pleasant and rug looks great. Non-stick feature is sprayed on rather than woven. Hope it holds up.
<Reasoning>Aesthetic praise with durability concern. Construction method doubt warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: So far this blender works well. Makes smooth smoothies with sharp design. Minor noise but excellent overall.
<Reasoning>Functional and aesthetic excellence with negligible drawback warrants 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: The towels are cute but not absorbent initially. After 2 washes with softener, they improve. Deducting one star.
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency mitigated by effort. Satisfied with reservation warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: Solves my scraping problem perfectly! Deducting one star because handle isn't dishwasher safe.
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with specific convenience drawback warrants 4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: Makes perfect smoothies but overheats constantly. Not reliable for daily use.
<Reasoning>Core functionality compromised by reliability issues despite good results.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: UPDATE: Worked great initially but broke after 2 months. Not worth the price.
<Reasoning>Negative update overrides initial positive experience. Short lifespan indicates poor quality.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: These mats work fine if replaced monthly. Great for guests but not daily durability.
<Reasoning>Conditional utility with explicit limitations. Temporary use cases balance to neutral.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: Catastrophic failure after 1 week. Dangerous sparks - immediate return!
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides any potential positives.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:

  gold 1 -> 2: 2
  gold 1 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."

  2. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."

  3. Review: "I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar or a can. This definitely takes care of that problem and with ease. Pour out what you can and simply use this to scrape the rest out. The reason I'm deducting a star is because it isn'..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."

  5. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I bought one of those Pyrex sets a few years ago.  You know, the one that came with the blue lids and some of the containers were rectangular --large and small-- while others were round (like these).  Well, turns out that the ones I used the most wer..."

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "If there's anything you ever thought you wanted or needed in a trash can, this one has it. First, it's a semi-round, so it seems to have a smaller footprint than traditional round cans. Second, it has a silent opening and soft closing, which is great..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 31 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
